reasoning: 1,1-dimethoxytrimethylamine (also known as trimethyl orthoformate aminol or N,N-dimethylformamide dimethyl acetal) has the structure (CH3O)2CH-N(CH3)2, featuring a carbon bearing both two alkoxy groups and a nitrogen, which is characteristic of an aminal (a carbon bonded to both an oxygen and a nitrogen, specifically an orthoamide/aminal structure). It fits best as an aminal within organonitrogen compounds.
